/tmp/story-13-final.md
# Story 13 â€” Metric Artifact Type â€” Final Plan (post-codex)

## Diagnosis (unchanged from /tmp/story-13-plan.md)

The artifact-type vision for MET-* is half-built:
- FEAT-005 lists MET as a conventional type; docgraph is type-agnostic.
- `ddx metric validate/run/compare/history/trend` already key off MET-* IDs.
- `ddx.execution.metric.metric_id` already binds gates to metrics.
- TD-005 already says metric history persists through `exec-runs`.
- What's missing: no checked-in MET-* artifacts, no metric-specific frontmatter
  schema, no library template bundle, `ddx doc validate` does not enforce
  metric_id resolution, no `ddx metric list` / `show`, no versioning policy.

## Metric artifact schema (revised after codex)

```yaml
---
ddx:
  id: MET-007
  depends_on: [FEAT-014, SD-016]
metric:
  schema_version: 1                # ADDED: codex #4 â€” schema compatibility
  unit: ms                         # required, free-form
  direction: lower-is-better       # MUST match ComparisonLowerIsBetter / ComparisonHigherIsBetter
  goal: 250                        # RENAMED from `target` â€” aspirational/documentary
  budget: 400                      # ADDED â€” machine-checkable hard line; gate ratchet authoritative when both set
  source: exec                     # v1 enum: exec | external (codex #7 â€” derived dropped from v1)
  scope: per-attempt               # per-attempt | per-bead | per-feature | global
---
```

Notes:
- `direction` enum strings exactly match Go constants in
  `cli/internal/metric/types.go` (`lower-is-better`, `higher-is-better`).
- `goal` vs `budget` distinction (codex #2): goal = aspirational, budget =
  hard line. Gate `thresholds.ratchet` remains authoritative for landing
  decisions. `ddx doc validate` emits a **warning** (not error) when MET
  budget conflicts with the gate ratchet that observes it (codex #3).
- `source: derived` is **out of v1** (codex #7). Reintroduce only when a
  real derivation schema exists.
- Multi-node semantics (codex #5): MET-* is project-local. History rows
  in `exec-runs` may be partitioned per-node by routing metadata; `ddx
  metric show` reports partitions when present, never silently aggregates.
- Mixed-unit history (codex #4): `compare` and `trend` refuse to mix
  units; `ddx metric history` shows all rows but groups by unit; old rows
  remain valid evidence.

## Run-step linkage (unchanged)

1. Gate â†’ metric: `ddx.execution.metric.metric_id: MET-NNN` (exists).
2. MET â†’ contributing gates: reverse index in `ddx metric show`.
3. AC â†’ MET citation: prose-only in v1 (codex #6 â€” defer structured
   `acceptance:` block until DDx validates bead ACs).

No new bead-event kinds. No new storage collection (codex #11; TD-005
confirms `exec-runs` already houses metric history).

## Spec changes

- **FEAT-005:** add Â§"Type-specific metadata example: MET" with the
  schema above (parallel to REF example).
- **TD-005:** brief amendment noting (a) `schema_version`, (b) mixed-unit
  refusal in compare/trend, (c) reverse-index for `metric show`.
- **FEAT-016:** cross-reference â€” process metrics rows decorated when
  ID matches an MET-* artifact.
- **FEAT-014:** brief note that runtime metrics MAY be promoted to MET-*.
- **No FEAT-020/021 changes** in v1 (multi-node aggregation deferred;
  per-MET dashboards are a follow-on).

## CLI surface (delta)

- `ddx metric list` â€” **add**; thin wrapper over `ddx doc list --type
  MET` joined with `metric.Store.History` for last-observed value/status.
- `ddx metric show <MET-id>` â€” **add**; frontmatter + reverse-index of
  observing gates + history partition by unit.
- `ddx doc validate` â€” **add rule**: every `execution.metric.metric_id`
  must resolve to an existing MET-* artifact (error); gate.ratchet vs
  MET.budget mismatch (warning).
- **Fix existing bug** (codex #1): `cli/cmd/metric.go:32`
  `resolveMetricDefinition` picks `defs[0]`; replace with
  `metric.Store.LoadDefinition` which already selects latest active
  deterministically.
- **Help text** (codex #1): clarify `ddx metric run` is a MET-ID
  convenience wrapper around `ddx exec run`.
- **README cleanup** (codex #14): the README claims
  `ddx metric define/log/result` exist â€” they do not. Trim the README
  to actual verbs (validate/run/compare/history/trend/list/show) as
  part of B3.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `library/artifacts/met/{template,create,evolve,check}.md` exists.
   Template renders the v1 schema (with `schema_version: 1`).
2. FEAT-005 documents the `metric:` block; TD-005 amended for
   schema_version + mixed-unit + reverse-index.
3. `ddx doc validate` errors when any
   `execution.metric.metric_id` does not resolve; warns when
   gate.ratchet conflicts with MET.budget. Test fixtures cover both
   cases.
4. `ddx metric list` returns MET-* docs with id, title, unit,
   direction, goal, budget, latest_value, latest_status; supports
   `--json`; empty-repo returns empty list (not error).
5. `ddx metric show MET-NNN` shows frontmatter + prose + observing gates
   + history partitioned by unit. `--json` shape documented.
6. â‰Ą2 real MET-* artifacts checked into `docs/metrics/`:
   - MET-001 (e.g. ddx-test-walltime, source=exec, goal+budget set)
   - MET-002 (e.g. cost-per-closed-bead, source=external, goal only)
   Each reachable from a root.
7. Integration test (codex #13): fixture project with
   `execution.metric.metric_id: MET-002` â†’ run â†’ `exec-runs` row
   appears â†’ `ddx metric show MET-002` lists the gate and the
   observation â†’ `ddx metric history MET-002` shows the value with
   correct unit.
8. `cli/cmd/metric.go:32` no longer uses `defs[0]`; uses
   `metric.Store.LoadDefinition`. Test pins deterministic selection.
9. README CLI command list trimmed to actually-implemented verbs.
10. `ddx metric compare` / `trend` refuse to operate on mixed-unit
    history (returns explicit error); test covers.

## Tests

- `cli/internal/docgraph/validate_test.go` â€” unresolved metric_id;
  valid metric_id; gate.ratchet vs MET.budget mismatch warning.
- `cli/internal/metric/store_test.go` â€” reverse-index resolution;
  mixed-unit refusal in Compare/Trend; partition-by-unit in History.
- `cli/cmd/metric_acceptance_test.go` â€” `ddx metric list`,
  `ddx metric show` golden output (text + --json), deterministic
  definition selection.
- New fixture: `cli/cmd/testdata/met-artifacts/` with two MET docs +
  gate doc referencing each.
- Integration round-trip test under `cli/cmd/` exercising
  gateâ†’runâ†’exec-runsâ†’show pipeline.
- No new e2e for v1; web UI deferred.

## Risks

- Schema lock-in too early â€” mitigated by `schema_version: 1`.
- Two sources of truth (MET.budget vs gate.ratchet) â€” gate authoritative;
  validate emits warning on conflict; revisit if drift becomes painful.
- Reverse-index cost â€” defer cache until measured.
- Deferred derived/multi-node/AC-comparator work could fragment if
  follow-on stories don't pick them up. Track in a "MET v2" backlog note
  on FEAT-005 (one paragraph, not a separate bead).

## Bead breakdown (revised â€” split B1 per codex #12)

- **B1a â€” FEAT-005 amendment + library/artifacts/met bundle**
  - Amend FEAT-005 with Â§"Type-specific metadata example: MET".
  - Amend TD-005 with schema_version + mixed-unit + reverse-index notes.
  - Create `library/artifacts/met/{template,create,evolve,check}.md`.
  - AC: docs render; templates install via `ddx init`; existing tests pass.
  - Tier: cheap.

- **B1b â€” Author first MET-* artifacts** (deps: B1a)
  - Author MET-001 (exec-source, e.g. ddx test wall time) with goal
    + budget; wire to an existing gate.
  - Author MET-002 (external-source, e.g. cost-per-closed-bead) with
    goal only.
  - Both reachable from a root.
  - AC: `ddx doc validate` clean; `ddx doc list --type MET` returns 2.
  - Tier: cheap (deciding real values is judgment-light here; pick
    plausible starting numbers).

- **B2 â€” `ddx doc validate` enforces metric_id; warns on
  budget/ratchet conflict** (deps: B1a)
  - Add rule + warning + test fixtures.
  - AC: dangling metric_id errors; valid passes; ratchet/budget
    conflict warns; `--json` surfaces error/warning codes.
  - Tier: cheap.

- **B3 â€” `ddx metric list` + `show` + bug fix + README cleanup**
  (deps: B1a, B1b)
  - Implement list and show; reverse-index over docgraph; partition by
    unit; deterministic-definition fix at metric.go:32.
  - Trim README CLI command list.
  - Update help text on `ddx metric run` to clarify wrapper semantics.
  - AC: golden tests pass; `--json` shapes documented; empty-repo safe;
    metric.go:32 uses LoadDefinition; README accurate.
  - Tier: standard.

- **B4 â€” Integration round-trip + mixed-unit refusal**
  (deps: B1b, B3)
  - Add integration test gateâ†’runâ†’exec-runsâ†’show.
  - Add Compare/Trend mixed-unit refusal + History partition tests.
  - AC: tests pass; failure modes have clear error messages.
  - Tier: standard.

- **B5 â€” FEAT-014 / FEAT-016 cross-reference amendments**
  (deps: B1a)
  - Brief edits noting MET-* promotion path.
  - AC: spec docs updated; no code change.
  - Tier: cheap.

(Web UI per-MET page, derived metrics with a real formula schema,
multi-node aggregation, structured AC comparator, and any default
MET-ESCALATION-* metric remain explicitly out of Story 13. Track in
a "MET v2" backlog note on FEAT-005 per codex #10.)

## Coordination

metric.go:32 uses LoadDefinition; README accurate.
  - Tier: standard.

- **B4 â€” Integration round-trip + mixed-unit refusal**
  (deps: B1b, B3)
  - Add integration test gateâ†’runâ†’exec-runsâ†’show.
  - Add Compare/Trend mixed-unit refusal + History partition tests.
  - AC: tests pass; failure modes have clear error messages.
  - Tier: standard.

- **B5 â€” FEAT-014 / FEAT-016 cross-reference amendments**
  (deps: B1a)
  - Brief edits noting MET-* promotion path.
  - AC: spec docs updated; no code change.
  - Tier: cheap.

(Web UI per-MET page, derived metrics with a real formula schema,
multi-node aggregation, structured AC comparator, and any default
MET-ESCALATION-* metric remain explicitly out of Story 13. Track in
a "MET v2" backlog note on FEAT-005 per codex #10.)

## Coordination

- **Story 11** (codex #9): avoid name collision. Story 11's GraphQL
  resolver should be named like `agentRoutingMetrics`; Story 13's
  surface uses `metricArtifacts` / `metricHistory`. Story 13 ships
  CLI only; web UI per-MET page is a future story consuming the same
  `exec-runs` source Story 11 already reads.
- **Story 10** (codex #10): no default MET-ESCALATION-* in this story.
  Add a one-line example in `library/artifacts/met/create.md` showing
  how a project would author one.
- **ADR-004** (codex #11): unchanged; `exec-runs` is the storage.
  No `metric-runs` rename.

## Flagged ambiguities (post-reconciliation)

1. **`source: external`** â€” what counts? GitHub API? A user-imported CSV?
   v1 leaves this prose-defined; B1b's MET-002 will set the precedent.
2. **`goal` vs `budget` semantics** â€” pinning gate.ratchet as
   authoritative is a v1 call; if many projects start writing MET.budget
   and ignoring gate.ratchet, we'll need to flip the authority. Revisit
   after 3 months of usage.
3. **Per-node partitioning shape** â€” `ddx metric show` reports
   partitions when routing metadata is present. The partition key
   (provider? harness? machine hostname?) is unspecified; B3 will need
   to pick one. Recommend: `provider/model` (matches Story 11's axes).
4. **Versioning beyond `schema_version: 1`** â€” when `direction` flips
   from lower-is-better to higher-is-better, is that a new MET-* ID or
   a schema_version bump? Recommend new ID; document in
   `library/artifacts/met/evolve.md`.

input.content:
Bead ddx-921616ea is the Story 13 epic. Its sole acceptance criterion is
"All child beads closed." Six children carry the testable ACs:

  - ddx-41481396  B1a: FEAT-005/TD-005 amendments + library/artifacts/met/ bundle
  - ddx-88507ed5  B1b: author MET-001 (exec) and MET-002 (external) artifacts
  - ddx-d75ae69a  B2:  ddx doc validate enforces metric_id; budget/ratchet warning
  - ddx-70467059  B3:  ddx metric list/show + metric.go:32 fix + README cleanup
  - ddx-296019fe  B4:  integration round-trip + mixed-unit refusal
  - ddx-cd42fc05  B5:  FEAT-014/FEAT-016 cross-reference edits

All six are currently open (verified via `ddx bead list --where
parent=ddx-921616ea`). Until they close, the epic's AC cannot be
satisfied â€” there is no implementation work owned by the epic itself.

What is done: nothing in this attempt; epic is purely a roll-up.

What is blocking: completion of the six children listed above. They
should be drained in dependency order (B1a â†’ B1b/B2 â†’ B3 â†’ B4; B5
parallel to B1a).

What a follow-up attempt would need: re-queue this epic only after all
six children are closed. At that point closing the epic is a one-line
status flip with no code change.

data.text: Epic ddx-921616ea is a roll-up; AC is "all children closed" and all 6 children remain open. Wrote no_changes rationale enumerating the children and the dependency order. No code changes.
